verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic face of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its catalytic and SH2/SH3 domains face the cytosol where it phosphorylates ITAMs of BCR/FcR and other inner-leaflet substrates. Not cell-state induced to outer leaflet; no tissue-restricted ecto display; no lysosomal exocytosis route; no dual outer-leaflet pool documented; not secreted with surface attachment. Therapeutic programs (e.g., bafetinib) are intracellular small-molecule kinase inhibitors, not surface binders.
